scope.summary: Java CompletableFuture and import-backed CompletionStage-style continuation calls now report shared future, task, aggregate, callback, and exception obligations.

capability_alignment.capability: map Java future evidence onto reusable cross-language runtime obligations
not_a_feature_list: No new SourceProtocolKind, SourceCallKind, semantic-pack row, or exact admission path was added.
CompletableFuture-specific selectors are mapped onto reusable future-settled, continuation, callback, task, aggregate, and exception obligations.
Simple CompletableFuture names require fully qualified syntax, exact import evidence, or conflict-free java.util.concurrent wildcard import evidence.
Continuation methods require receiver-domain FutureLike evidence backed by exact java.util.concurrent CompletableFuture or CompletionStage import evidence instead of selector-only attribution.
Broad CompletableFuture mentions, constructors, Executor/Future APIs, untyped receivers, and local or conflicting type bindings remain closed.
reused_capabilities: Java imported binding and wildcard import evidence
Java import-backed parameter type-domain evidence for CompletableFuture and CompletionStage receivers
shared task, async aggregate, callback demand/effect, exception-channel, and future settled-value missing-evidence labels

new_reporting_surfaces.guard: The CompletableFuture type must be fully qualified, exact-imported from java.util.concurrent, or available through conflict-free java.util.concurrent.* before the call.
The CompletableFuture type must have the same import or fully qualified identity proof as async factories.
The CompletableFuture type must have the same import or fully qualified identity proof as async factories.

new_reporting_surfaces.surface: CompletionStage-style receiver continuations such as thenApply, exceptionally, handle, whenComplete, thenCombine, and applyToEither
new_reporting_surfaces.guard: The receiver must have FutureLike domain evidence backed by an exact java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture or java.util.concurrent.CompletionStage import dependency.

assertions: CompletableFuture.supplyAsync/runAsync report task, future-settled, callback, and exception obligations.
completedFuture and failedFuture report settled-value channels while failedFuture also reports the exception channel.
allOf reports all-completion aggregate obligations and anyOf reports first-completion aggregate obligations.
Import-backed CompletableFuture receiver thenApply reports fulfillment continuation and callback obligations.
Import-backed CompletableFuture receiver exceptionally reports exception continuation and exception-channel obligations.
Import-backed CompletionStage receiver handle reports settlement continuation obligations.
Import-backed CompletableFuture receiver applyToEither reports first-completion aggregate obligations.
Unimported simple CompletableFuture names do not report task-spawn obligations.
A local CompletableFuture type blocks stdlib future attribution.
A local parameter named CompletableFuture blocks stdlib future attribution.
A conflicting explicit import blocks wildcard CompletableFuture attribution.
An untyped receiver named future.thenApply does not report future continuation obligations.
A local CompletableFuture receiver type does not report future continuation obligations.
A custom imported CompletionStage receiver type does not report future continuation obligations.
A wildcard-only CompletableFuture receiver type stays closed until type-domain evidence is import-backed.
A java.util.concurrent.* wildcard import remains usable even if another Java file has an exact CompletableFuture import.
A java.util.concurrent.* wildcard import remains usable even if another Java file has a conflicting CompletableFuture import.

corpus_pricing_delta.reason: The lexical pricing script now splits Java future API candidate surfaces out of the broad CompletableFuture mention bucket. The runtime reporter covers proof-backed subsets of those surfaces, while broad residual CompletableFuture mentions fall from 306 to 276.

release_verify_timing.baseline_reference: Swift structured-concurrency release verify warm median real_seconds = 0.98
release_verify_timing.assessment: No large degradation observed; the changed path adds Java-only attribution and import-backed type-domain checks, and the crates workload contains no Java source.

next_exact_work_policy.summary: Java future surfaces are now attributable for recall-loss reporting, but exact recovery still requires dependency-closed executor timing, callback identity/effects, cancellation/liveness, exceptional completion, and result-channel contracts.
